01 — My Story

Built From
the Ground Up

Jerzy Adams · Eller College

View LinkedIn Profile

I grew up in Los Gatos, California — where I learned early that nothing comes without putting in the work. As a first-generation college student, I arrived at the University of Arizona's Eller College of Management without a roadmap. What I had instead was discipline, curiosity, and a relentless drive to prove that I belong in every room I walk into.

Gymnastics shaped me before business did. Being a gymnast taught me how to perform under pressure, absorb setbacks quickly, and push through when everything hurts. When my time as an athlete ended, those lessons didn't — they followed me directly into coaching, where I led a competitive team of athletes and recreational classes, learning what it means to develop people, not just performance.

As I transitioned into college, I started to realize that leadership looks different in every environment—but the core of it stays the same. Whether I was organizing recruitment behind the scenes or stepping into new professional spaces, I found myself drawn to roles where I could bring structure, energy, and clarity to fast-moving situations. I've learned that I do my best work when I'm trusted with responsibility and when the outcome truly matters.

That mindset became even more real for me this past summer, when I stepped into a completely new environment and had to learn quickly, ask better questions, and hold myself to a higher standard. Being in that kind of setting pushed me to think more strategically, communicate more confidently, and understand how much impact strong relationships and attention to detail can have on real outcomes. It gave me a clearer picture of the kind of career I want to build and the kind of professional I want to become.

I'm not just building a résumé. I'm building a career with intention — and this website is the story of how I got here.

My Story in Motion

South Bay Construction · Campbell, CA

Bascom Station: A Full-Circle Moment

My internship at South Bay Construction put me at the center of one of the Bay Area's most active job sites. Assigned to Bascom Station — a $152M, nearly 449,000 SF development that transitioned from a Class A office concept into a fully operational medical-grade facility for Valley Health Care — I contributed to project coordination, construction accounting, and subcontractor management across every phase. I handled change orders, insurance tracking, site walks, MOB workbooks, and weekly OAC meetings. Working alongside both office and field teams gave me a ground-level understanding of how precision, communication, and adaptability drive outcomes on large-scale, complex projects — and earned me an invitation to return for a second summer.

02 — Persona Evolution

Who I Think I Am
vs. Who You See

This section documents my professional growth through data I've collected, analyzed, and reflected on — a Self-Perception Inventory, a Professional Reputation Survey, and 12 Team 360° evaluations. Together they tell the story of the gap between identity and reputation — and what I'm doing to close it.

Core Values

The Five Principles Behind Every Decision

Click each value to learn how it shows up in my work.

L Leadership

I step up before being asked. From organizing team meetings to volunteering for the hardest presentation section — leadership means setting the standard, not waiting for permission.

A Accountability

I hold myself to a high standard and I'm working to extend that same directness to the people around me. Accountability is how teams stay on track and how trust gets built.

R Resilience

Eleven years of gymnastics taught me to fall and get back up — fast. I showed up prepared to a practice presentation while sick. Resilience isn't toughness; it's not stopping.

I Impact

I measure success by what changes because I showed up. A national recruitment record. A 1st-place business pitch. Athletes who believe in themselves. Impact is the only metric that matters.

C Connection

Business development and real estate run on relationships. I invest in people genuinely — as a coach, a teammate, and a professional. Connection is how I turn strategy into results.

Behavioral Communication Style

The Driver

What this means

Results-
First.

In my BCOM Self-Perception Inventory — a structured tool for understanding how behavioral tendencies shape professional relationships — I was identified as a Driver. Drivers are direct, goal-oriented, and task-focused. We lead with action and measure success by outcomes.

For a non-specialist: the Driver style is the person who already knows what needs to happen and focuses on making it happen — efficiently and with high standards. The risk is moving so fast you leave people behind. The strength is that things actually get done.

Evidence from my experience

I apply my Driver tendencies daily as part of a 6-person collaborative team across 3 major upper-division courses — each with distinct deliverables, timelines, and group dynamics.

Set the team's meeting cadence and kept it consistent
Took on an additional presentation section requiring more preparation
Addressed performance gaps directly when the team needed it
Came to every meeting with work already done
Sought additional feedback from instructors to strengthen team output
Delivered at full effort even while managing personal challenges

Growth edge

Multiple 360 reviewers noted I could be more direct about holding teammates accountable and lean on the team more — a classic Driver growth pattern I'm actively working on.

Identity vs. Reputation

What I See vs. What You Experience

How I see myself

Driven

Internal force — "I push myself relentlessly."

Self-Confident

Internal state — "I trust my judgment."

Outgoing

Social identity — "I connect easily with people."

→

How teammates see me

Proactive Leader

Behavioral — "She organizes before being asked."

Reliable

Observed — "She shows up, even when sick."

Goes Above & Beyond

Team impact — "She did more than her own slide."

The most meaningful finding: my self-perception is trait-focused (who I am internally) while my reputation is behavior-focused (what I do for others). I see myself as driven — my teammates feel the impact of that drive in how the team performs. The growth challenge is applying the same intentionality to accountability: being as direct with feedback as I am with effort.

Team 360° Analysis

12 Reviews. One Clear Pattern.

Positive Sentiment

In Their Own Words

"Jerzy always came to meetings with ideas for the slides and was very proactive in setting up when we should meet. She also did the introduction, taking on more of the work than just her own slide. She is very positive and is very helpful to everyone in the group. I feel like she improved a ton in a lot of aspects since the first two presentations."

Team 360° EvaluationSentiment: 0.97

"Jerzy is a very active contributor to our team who goes above and beyond. While everyone does a great job ensuring their individual pieces are done, Jerzy makes an effort to check that everyone's pieces are complete. Jerzy is extremely communicative and is not scared to speak her mind — which is extremely helpful for our team as a whole."

Team 360° EvaluationSentiment: 0.95

"Jerzy helped organize many of our meetings and did a great job communicating with our preceptor. She also did a good job communicating with the group when she felt people weren't putting in effort — and this shows how much she cares about the team's success. Overall, Jerzy has done a great job stepping up when the team needs her."

Team 360° EvaluationSentiment: 0.99

"Jerzy consistently brought a positive attitude toward our group and was very active in the group chat, making sure communication stayed clear. She also took the initiative to coordinate multiple meetings, even when she wasn't feeling well — which showed her dedication to the team's success."

Team 360° EvaluationSentiment: 0.94

"You bring great energy to the team, and when you step up and take the lead, it really sets the tone for everyone else. Your reliability and willingness to guide the group helps the group be successful."

Team 360° EvaluationSentiment: 0.93

Growth Over Time

Charting the Arc

Early Semester

Self-Perception Inventory

Completed structured assessments of communication style, work preferences, and core values. Identified as a Driver — direct, results-oriented, high-urgency. This became the baseline for everything that followed.

Mid Semester

Professional Reputation Survey

Surveyed peers and mentors. Results showed strong alignment on Drive and Confidence — but revealed a reputation defined by behaviors and team impact rather than internal traits. Others don't just see my drive; they feel its effect.

Late Semester

Team 360° Evaluations

Received 12 anonymous evaluations. Average sentiment: 0.88/1.0. Consistent themes: proactive leadership, reliability, positive energy, strong communication. Consistent growth area: more direct accountability and delegating instead of absorbing.

Going Forward

The Persona I'm Building

Entering business development and real estate with a clear picture of both strengths and growth edges. My goal: lead with the warmth and reliability my teammates already see — and layer in the direct accountability that will make me a stronger professional.

03 — Portfolio

The Work
Speaks for Itself

Three projects that represent the range of my skills — from data analysis and visualization to strategic communication and client-facing business development.

01

Client Strategy · Group Project · BCOM 314

Native: Client Communication Plan

Situation: Native faced a perception gap between their brand and how consumers interpreted their messaging on price and sustainability.

My role: Led Background and Stakeholder Analysis sections. Delivered live pitch to a panel of executives. Coordinated team throughout all three presentation phases.

Result: Comprehensive 3-phase Instagram influencer strategy with projected 10–20% engagement lift and $75K–$100K campaign plan.

Stakeholder Analysis Strategy Deck Executive Pitch Market Research

View Project →

02

Data Visualization · Solo Authored · BNAN 277

Dept. of Education: Graduation Analysis

Situation: Final project analyzing U.S. graduation rate trends across the Department of Education dataset using Tableau.

My role: Sole author. Built interactive multi-sheet story in Tableau, identifying patterns across institutions, demographics, and time periods.

Skills: Tableau, data storytelling, statistical analysis, information design. Live and interactive — click to explore.

Tableau Data Viz Solo Authored Education Sector

03

Business Development · Competition · 1st Place

BNAD 200 Business Case Competition

Situation: Design a growth strategy for a local coffee shop, pitched live to a panel of judges.

Result: 1st place out of 500+ participants across 100 competitive teams. Proposal included full financial modeling, market segmentation, and facility flow optimization.

Skills: Feasibility analysis, revenue projection, strategic storytelling, data visualization under competitive pressure.

05 — Looking Ahead

Where I'm
Headed

I'm entering business development and real estate with a clear sense of direction — roles where strategy, relationships, and performance intersect. Here's where I'm focused next.

Summer 2026 — Confirmed

South Bay Construction — Year 2

Returning to South Bay Construction for my second summer, deepening my experience in the construction and real estate industry. Building on last year's foundation — stronger relationships, sharper skills, and a clearer sense of where I'm headed.

Long Term

Leadership in Real Estate

My long-term goal is to grow into a leadership role in real estate — building a career that combines strategic business development, client relationships, and high-performance execution. I want to create impact not just for my clients, but for the communities I work in.

Target Industries & Roles

Where My Skills Create the Most Value

Commercial Real Estate

Brokerage, acquisitions, development, or asset management. Firms like CBRE, JLL, Colliers, and Cushman & Wakefield.

Business Development

Sales strategy, client acquisition, partnership development. High-growth companies where relationships drive revenue.

Corporate Strategy

Growth planning, market analysis, and execution. Roles that combine analytical thinking with cross-functional leadership.
